meta.note: subscription auth; codex reports tokens, not USD

A.missing: Drops secure attachment or resistance to separation; carries only prolonged contact.
A.reason: Causing one thing to touch another for a long time also describes many non-fastening actions.

B.missing: Drops the caused secure relation and wrongly asserts that neither thing moves at all.
B.reason: Fastened things can move together, and the clauses do not connect the actions causally to attachment.

C.missing: Wrongly makes the things absolutely incapable of ceasing to touch.
C.reason: It sharply represents an action on two things yielding attachment, but fastening does not entail irreversible inseparability.

D.missing: Drops relative resistance to separation and instead says one thing cannot move far after now.
D.reason: The absolute movement restriction is neither properly tied to the other thing nor criterial of fastening.

E.missing: Drops the genus of an intentional act and carries only an unspecified event producing proximity.
E.reason: An event after which two things touch and cannot be far apart need not be an act of fastening.

F.missing: Drops intentional action and wrongly requires prior noncontact and absolute inability of one thing to move far.
F.reason: It depicts a change into constrained contact but not specifically someone's act of fastening two things together.

G.missing: Drops the resulting secure attachment or resistance to separation; carries only action involving two touching things.
G.reason: Someone doing something with two things while they touch is far too generic to identify fastening.
